input.rootCause: Vercel's deploy gate rejected the build because package.json pins next to ^16.0.1, a version Vercel flags as vulnerable; the build itself completed successfully before the platform refused to publish it.
input.narrative:
I read the log tail and saw the build finished cleanly ("Build Completed in /vercel/output [48s]") but Vercel then emitted "Vulnerable version of Next.js detected" and failed the deploy. I grepped package.json and confirmed the dependency is pinned at "next": "^16.0.1", which resolved to 16.0.1 in the build — a release Vercel currently blocks for a known CVE. The fix lives in application dependencies, outside Convoy-authored files.</narrative>
<parameter name="reproduction">npx vercel deploy --yes
input.description: Bump the next dependency in package.json to a patched release that Vercel no longer flags (e.g. the latest 16.x patch — run `npm i next@latest` and commit the updated package.json and package-lock.json), then redeploy. Nothing in vercel.json, .env.schema, or .convoy/manifest.yaml can bypass this platform-side security gate; the version pin must change.

I read the log tail and saw the build finished cleanly ("Build Completed in /vercel/output [48s]") but Vercel then emitted "Vulnerable version of Next.js detected" and failed the deploy. I grepped package.json and confirmed the dependency is pinned at "next": "^16.0.1", which resolved to 16.0.1 in the build — a release Vercel currently blocks for a known CVE. The fix lives in application dependencies, outside Convoy-authored files.

toolCalls.name: finalize_diagnosis

input.rootCause: Vercel's deploy gate rejected the build because package.json pins next to ^16.0.1, a version Vercel flags as vulnerable; the build itself completed successfully before the platform refused to publish it.
input.narrative:
I read the log tail and saw the build finished cleanly ("Build Completed in /vercel/output [48s]") but Vercel then emitted "Vulnerable version of Next.js detected" and failed the deploy. I grepped package.json and confirmed the dependency is pinned at "next": "^16.0.1", which resolved to 16.0.1 in the build — a release Vercel currently blocks for a known CVE. The fix lives in application dependencies, outside Convoy-authored files.</narrative>
<parameter name="reproduction">npx vercel deploy --yes
input.description: Bump the next dependency in package.json to a patched release that Vercel no longer flags (e.g. the latest 16.x patch — run `npm i next@latest` and commit the updated package.json and package-lock.json), then redeploy. Nothing in vercel.json, .env.schema, or .convoy/manifest.yaml can bypass this platform-side security gate; the version pin must change.

toolCalls.resultPreview: finalized: Vercel's deploy gate rejected the build because package.json pins next to ^16.0.1, a version Vercel flags as vulnerable;
